description: load_config() reads flat keys (pool_max_size, pool_idle_timeout, etc.) from YAML but transport_example.yaml uses nested keys (network.pool.max_size, network.pool.idle_timeout). All network config values silently default. Same mismatch for presentation (YAML has presentation.pipeline.serializer, loader reads pres.get('serializer')).
proposed_fix: Align load_config() key paths with nested YAML structure (e.g., net.get('pool', {}).get('max_size', 50) for network, pres.get('pipeline', {}).get('serializer', 'json') for presentation); add diff-warning log for unread keys

description: ICommunicationGateway protocol (communication_gateway.py:15-45) lacks liveness_check()/readiness_check() methods. FabricClient only has health_check(). FabricHealthEndpoints (fabric_health.py:56-59) calls gateway.liveness_check() and readiness_check() which will raise AttributeError at runtime. CommunicationFabricAssembler has both methods at lines 287 and 297 but they are not exposed through FabricClient or the protocol.
proposed_fix: Add liveness_check() and readiness_check() to FabricClient (delegate to Assembler's implementations); add both methods to ICommunicationGateway protocol

description: Imports 'from contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway' but the actual module is at wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py. No root-level contracts/ package exists — ModuleNotFoundError at import time.
proposed_fix: Change import to 'from wiring.contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway'

description: ProtocolEntryConfig expects transport/endpoint/port/socket_path fields but YAML protocol entries (http, graphql, websocket, grpc) have protocol-specific keys (enabled, base_url, ping_interval, max_depth, secure). All protocols silently default to transport='inprocess' with no endpoint.
proposed_fix: Align ProtocolEntryConfig with actual YAML structure — either read protocol-specific fields per type or add a transport sub-section per protocol in YAML; log warning for each unread protocol field

description: _build_protocol_handlers() creates inbound PresentationPipeline with hardcoded serializer_name='json' and compressor_name='gzip' at line 276-277, ignoring the loaded config values from cfg.presentation (which may have different serializer/compressor). This causes encode/decode asymmetry if outbound uses different settings.
proposed_fix: Reuse loaded PresentationConfig for the inbound pipeline: serializer_name=cfg.presentation.serializer, compressor_name=cfg.presentation.compressor
